name: Nina Simone
description: American singer, songwriter and pianist and civil rights activist (1933–2003)
abstract: Nina Simone ( NEE-nə sim-OHN; born Eunice Kathleen Waymon; February 21, 1933 – April 21, 2003) was an American composer, concert pianist, singer, songwriter, and civil rights activist. Simone's bearing and stage presence earned her the title the High Priestess of Soul.

about_quotes.speaker: John Beal
about_quotes.evidence: many years later I got to play on her record that she wrote, you know, in the string section of a record called “Baltimore.”
about_quotes.doc_title: John Beal interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, June 18, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Bobby Hutcherson
about_quotes.evidence: they suggested, we need a vibe player to play with Nina Simone, and they suggested that it be me.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bobby Hutcherson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Don Alias
about_quotes.evidence: I played drums on that job with Nina Simone and also some percussion.
about_quotes.doc_title: Don Alias interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 6, 2002

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: Nina Simone had the classic record on it in terms of like most of the singers who sang it sang the version based, their versions are based on her version. And her version is based on my instrumental version, which I did at the piano.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor interviewed by Michael Woods, New York City, New York, July 27, 1995

about_quotes.speaker: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola
about_quotes.evidence: I saw a heart wrenching interview... somebody interviewed Nina Simone after she had left the States and went to France I think when she came back and they were interviewing her.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dawoud Sabu Adeyola interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, January 30, 2025

about_quotes.speaker: Onaje Allan Gumbs
about_quotes.evidence: my sister also had an impact on me... she had Barbra Streisand... Nina Simone. So I would go down to the basement where all the music was and just listen to everything she had.
about_quotes.doc_title: Onaje Allan Gumbs interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 12, 2001
